9th Hour

The product is built.
Now it needs to work.

Temporary technical and product ownership for B2B software teams.

9th Hour is a personal studio for founders and teams who have successfully launched, but have lost the ability to move with confidence. I step in to resolve technical debt, clarify priorities, and restore a culture of predictable delivery.

The Context

The weight of a working product.

There is a specific point in a software product's life where the original momentum stalls. You have real users and real revenue, but development has become a source of friction rather than a lever for progress.

Features take longer to ship than they used to.

The roadmap is a collection of guesses rather than a strategy.

Decision fatigue is slowing down the founders and the engineering team.

The introduction of AI or new modules feels aimless and reactionary.

"You don't need more developers. You need a clear path and the authority to take it."

The Role

Temporary ownership, permanent clarity.

I do not act as an advisor or a consultant who leaves you with a document. I act as a temporary technical and product owner. I take responsibility for the difficult decisions that have been deferred.

Establish Structure

Implement the discipline required to turn 'messy' into 'maintainable.'

Drive Decisions

Remove the bottleneck of uncertainty by making technical and product trade-offs with confidence.

Restore Calm

Replace the anxiety of the unknown with a steady, predictable rhythm of shipping.

Bridge the Gap

Align the business goals of the founders with the technical realities of the code.

The Roadmap

A deliberate path toward stability.

01

Clarity

An immediate audit of the current technical state, team dynamics, and product bottlenecks. We identify exactly why progress has slowed.

02

Direction

We define a focused roadmap. We stop doing the things that don't matter and commit to the few things that do.

03

Delivery

I lead the team through the execution phase, resolving technical debt and shipping critical updates to prove the new rhythm works.

04

Handover

Once the product is stable and the team is aligned, I prepare the environment for your long-term technical leadership and step away.

9th Hour Is

  • One senior engineer taking full responsibility.
  • A temporary intervention (3–9 months).
  • Focused on discipline and craftsmanship.
  • Direct, honest, and decisive.

9th Hour Is Not

  • An agency looking to bill hours.
  • A recruitment or body-leasing service.
  • A source of generic 'advice.'
  • A permanent hire.
The Ideal Match

For products with real complexity.

This studio is designed for B2B founders and product owners who manage teams of 5 to 20 people.

Your product has an established user base and revenue.

The technical foundation feels fragile or overwhelming.

You feel like you are losing the 'big picture' to daily fires.

You value professional maturity over temporary hype.

The Principal

A discipline of delivery.

9th Hour is run by a senior software engineer with a career built on responsibility. I have seen how quickly software can become a burden when it lacks a steady hand.

I value quiet competence, long-term thinking, and the relief that comes from a well-ordered system. I don't believe in "moving fast and breaking things"—I believe in moving steadily so that things stay fixed.

Start a conversation.

If your product development no longer feels under your control, let's discuss the current state of your project. No sales pitch, just a professional evaluation of whether I can help you regain your momentum.

Typical response within 24 hours.

9th Hour
